Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Wait the prompt says If the first activity occurs then the second activity should have been executed previously So A B means if A occurs B must occur before A Lets stick to this definition

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Activities RO CA RI PI QC PO GSL D IC RP Process flow 1 RO happens exactly once 2 CA happens exactly once after RO 3 RI happens exactly once after CA 4 PI happens exactly once after RI 5 QC happens exactly once after PI 6 PO happens exactly once after QC 7 GSL happens exactly once after PO 8 D happens exactly once after GSL 9 IC happens exactly once after D 10 RP happens exactly once after IC

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Usually each of these activities happens exactly once per order

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: RO to CA maybe directly or maybe not Lets say directly

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: If they all directly follow then its a strict sequence Lets make it a bit more flexible Maybe IC can happen anytime after RO Lets just use a subset for directly follows

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always Before If the first activity occurs then the second activity should have been executed previously CA RO means RO before CA RI CA means CA before RI PI RI means RI before PI QC PI means PI before QC PO QC means QC before PO GSL PO means PO before GSL D GSL means GSL before D RP IC means IC before RP

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always After A B means B occurs after A RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D IC RP

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Equivalence Since all activities occur exactly once they are all equivalent in terms of occurrence But usually equivalence is used for activities that are alternatives or always happen together Lets leave it empty Empty is safer if we do not want to overconstrain

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Never Together Are there any mutually exclusive activities Maybe Cancel Order and Dispatch but Cancel Order is not in the list So set

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets review the prompt to ensure no specific constraints were given in the text that I missed There are no explicit constraints given in the text like A must happen before B it just describes the process flow I need to infer the constraints from the process description

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
